seoTitle: Move and Position Text Boxes Precisely in Power BI
metaDescription: Learn how to select, move, and precisely position a text box in Power BI so your report layouts look intentional instead of thrown together.
intro: A misaligned text box is a small thing that makes a whole report look unpolished, and Power BI's layout tools give you more control than most people realize. This guide covers selecting a text box, moving it, and adjusting its size and position for a clean, intentional layout. It's useful for anyone assembling reports or dashboards meant to be shared outside their own team.

steps.heading: Select the text box
steps.body: Click on the text box you want to reposition to bring up its selection handles and formatting options in the pane.

steps.heading: Move the text box
steps.body: Drag the text box to its new position, or use the position and size fields in the formatting pane for exact pixel placement.

steps.heading: Adjust layout for precision
steps.body: Use alignment guides or the formatting pane's numeric inputs to line the text box up evenly with other elements on the page.

steps.heading: Follow best practices for text box use
steps.body: Keep text boxes consistent in size and placement across similar reports so your dashboards feel cohesive as a set.

faq.question: How do I move a text box to an exact position in Power BI?
faq.answer: Select the text box and use the position (X, Y) fields in the formatting pane to enter exact coordinates rather than dragging it by hand.

faq.question: Can I lock a text box in place after positioning it?
faq.answer: Power BI doesn't have a direct lock feature for individual objects, but grouping elements together can help prevent accidental repositioning.

faq.question: Why does my text box snap to unexpected positions?
faq.answer: This is usually due to Power BI's snap-to-grid or snap-to-object behavior, which can be adjusted or disabled in the report's layout settings.

faq.question: Can text boxes be aligned with other visuals automatically?
faq.answer: Yes, selecting multiple objects lets you use the built-in align and distribute options to line text boxes up evenly with charts or other elements.
